desc: Copenhagen combines design-led neighborhoods, easy cycling infrastructure, and a compact harbor-centered layout that works exceptionally well for short city breaks. Late spring through early autumn brings the strongest outdoor rhythm, while December adds festive demand and pricing pressure around Christmas markets and winter events.
hls: Harbor swimming culture in warm months

todo.name: Nyhavn to Kongens Nytorv walk
todo.desc: Start at the iconic canal facades, then continue into central squares and shopping streets.

todo.name: Bike the harbor bridges and neighborhoods
todo.desc: Rent a city bike to connect Vesterbro, Nørrebro, and Christianshavn the local way.

todo.name: Rosenborg Castle and King’s Garden
todo.desc: Tour royal chambers and crown jewels, then relax in one of the city’s favorite green spaces.

todo.name: Torvehallerne food market
todo.desc: Sample smørrebrød, pastries, coffee, and seasonal Danish ingredients under one roof.

todo.name: Designmuseum Danmark or Louisiana Museum
todo.desc: Choose central design collections or a half-day coastal museum trip north of the city.

todo.name: Evening at Tivoli Gardens
todo.desc: Enjoy classic rides, lights, and concerts in the historic amusement park beside the central station.

wifi.note: Very reliable in hotels, cafés, coworking spaces, and major transport hubs; excellent for remote work.

prac.fltNote: Gatwick-Copenhagen is commonly nonstop year-round on multiple carriers. Return fares are often ~£45-£120 off-peak, ~£120-£220 in shoulder demand, and ~£220+ during summer peaks and major December market weeks.
prac.visa: Denmark is in Schengen; UK and US passport holders can generally stay visa-free for up to 90 days in any 180-day period.
alerts: Hotel pricing can rise sharply during summer festivals, major congress dates, and high-demand design/food events.
Popular dining reservations can require booking several weeks ahead in peak weekends.
Winter weather is manageable but windy waterfront conditions can make long walking routes feel colder than forecast highs.

sweet: May-June and September are usually the best-balanced months for long days, active waterfront life, and lower pricing pressure than midsummer peaks.
